vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2173: bug(flywheel): under-plans new ready+released backlog while busy draining a cohort

plan.title: Flywheel planning floor — plan new ready backlog while draining a cohort
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: RUN-37/38 (opus, 2026-06-29): the flywheel spent every tick draining the in-flight cohort and never planned the new ready backlog — PAN-1894 and PAN-2076 sat unplanned for hours despite free capacity (3-7/20) and no objection/parked labels. Two root causes: (1) roles/flywheel.md step 5 has only soft prose ('keep planning aggressively') with no enforceable per-tick floor, and the PAN-2006 cohort-freeze wording biases the LLM toward drain-only; (2) the needsPlanning set is computed only as a COUNT (computeStats.needsPlanning) and never surfaced as a LIST the orchestrator reads each tick — GET /api/backlog/forecast returns inFlight/waves/cohort but unplanned-ready issues appear in none of them. A soft-prose-only fix would not work: RUN-37/38 already had the soft prose.
narratives.Proposal: Make the planning floor data-driven and mandated, reusing the existing pure pickup model. (1) Add selectNeedsPlanning(nodes, lk, {cap}) in src/lib/backlog/pickup.ts mirroring selectUnblockTargets — returns the ranked needsPlanning issues. (2) Expose a needsPlanning[] list in GET /api/backlog/forecast (src/dashboard/server/routes/backlog.ts) alongside inFlight/waves/cohort. (3) Add an explicit 'Planning floor' rule to roles/flywheel.md step 5: each tick read needsPlanning[] and run `pan plan <id> --auto` for up to 2 that pass the author/assignee + parked/objection gates, EVEN while draining the cohort; reconcile with PAN-2006 (planning fills awaiting-release, never auto-starts, never extends the cohort); state the within-1-2-ticks SLA.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No change to existing forecast fields, stats.needsPlanning count semantics, or the Forecast UI.
- No author/assignee filtering inside the forecast endpoint — it stays a pure model projection; the role applies the security gate per item.
- No new `pan` CLI subcommand — reuse the existing forecast API + `pan plan --auto`.
- No auto-START of planned work — the PAN-2059 Release gate is unchanged; the floor only PLANS (`pan plan --auto`, never `--auto-start`).
- No change to cohort-freeze / run-completion logic (PAN-2006 computeCohort / saveRunCohort).
- No code path that auto-fires planning agents — the orchestrator remains the sole launcher.

autoDecisions.summary: Floor cap = 2 planning launches per tick.
autoDecisions.rationale: Mirrors selectUnblockTargets' default cap of 2; small enough to coexist with cohort drain under the maxAgents ceiling, large enough to keep the awaiting-release queue from starving.

autoDecisions.summary: The floor applies regardless of auto_pickup_backlog.
autoDecisions.rationale: Planning stops at the PAN-2059 Release gate and never auto-starts, so it cannot bypass operator control; step 5 already encourages aggressive planning unconditionally.

autoDecisions.summary: The forecast endpoint exposes needsPlanning UN-filtered by author/assignee; the role applies the security gate.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps the endpoint a pure projection of the shared pickup model (consistent with stats/waves/cohort) and puts the security gate where roles/flywheel.md already enforces it per launch, avoiding divergent gating logic in two places.

autoDecisions.summary: No code auto-launches planning agents; surface + mandate only.
autoDecisions.rationale: The orchestrator's #1 job is launching agents; a second uncontrolled launcher would race the LLM and the governor. Surfacing the list + a mandated rule fixes the root cause without a parallel launch path.

items.title: Add selectNeedsPlanning pure selector + unit test

narrative.Action: In src/lib/backlog/pickup.ts add `selectNeedsPlanning(nodes, lk, opts?: { cap?: number }): ForecastNode[]`, mirroring the existing `selectUnblockTargets` (pickup.ts ~:215). Map each node to { issue, rank, size, state: classifyIssue(node, lk) }, keep only the needsPlanning predicate already used by computeStats (pickup.ts:252): `s.ready && !s.planned && !s.parked && !s.vetoed && !s.objection && !s.inPipeline`, sort by rank ascending, and slice to cap (default 2, min 1, like selectUnblockTargets). Add unit tests in src/lib/backlog/__tests__/pickup.test.ts in a new `describe('selectNeedsPlanning')` block using the existing `node()` / `lookups()` helpers (mirror the selectUnblockTargets describe block at ~:173).

items.title: selectNeedsPlanning returns only ready-but-unplanned issues (ready && !planned), in ascending rank order
selectNeedsPlanning excludes issues that are planned, parked, vetoed, objected, or in-pipeline (returns none of them even when ready)
selectNeedsPlanning respects the cap option: with cap 1 it returns at most the single lowest-rank needsPlanning issue; defaults to cap 2 when omitted
selectNeedsPlanning returns an empty array when no issue satisfies the needsPlanning predicate

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/backlog.ts forecast handler (~:292), import selectNeedsPlanning and compute `const needsPlanning = selectNeedsPlanning(nodes, lk, { cap: n * 2 }).map(enrich)` (reuse the existing `enrich` closure that adds title/importance/score/why, same as inFlight/waves). Add `needsPlanning` to the returned JSON object alongside the existing `inFlight`, `waves`, `lanes`, `cohort`, `stats`, `epics`, `contains` keys. Also add it (as `[]`) to the early empty-sequence return so the response shape is stable when sequence.md is absent. Do NOT modify any existing field or stats.needsPlanning.

items.title: GET /api/backlog/forecast returns a response containing a `needsPlanning` array of enriched nodes ({ issue, rank, title, ... }) derived from selectNeedsPlanning
When sequence.md is absent the forecast handler returns `needsPlanning: []` (stable shape)
Existing forecast fields (stats, inFlight, waves, lanes, cohort, epics, contains) are unchanged — typecheck passes and no existing field is renamed or removed
Add the Planning floor rule to roles/flywheel.md step 5

narrative.Action: Extend step 5 of roles/flywheel.md (the 'Launch agents aggressively' step, ~line 110) with an explicit 'Planning floor' rule. It must state: (a) each tick, call `GET /api/backlog/forecast` and read its `needsPlanning[]` list; (b) run `pan plan <id> --auto` (NEVER `--auto-start`) for up to 2 of those items, EVEN while draining the in-flight cohort; (c) apply the same author/assignee gate (verify via `gh issue view <num> --json author,assignees`) and skip parked/objection items before planning each one; (d) only plan while total active agents stays under maxAgents (capacity check); (e) the PAN-2006 reconciliation — planning fills the awaiting-release queue, does NOT auto-start, and does NOT extend the running cohort, so it is orthogonal to cohort drain; (f) the SLA — a ready, vetted, capacity-available issue is planned within ~1-2 ticks, not stranded for hours. Keep operator-facing wording self-contained (define needsPlanning/Release-gate inline) per the operator-message rule. Do not renumber other steps.

items.title: roles/flywheel.md step 5 contains a 'Planning floor' rule directing the orchestrator to read forecast `needsPlanning[]` and run `pan plan <id> --auto` (not --auto-start) for up to 2 items per tick
The rule contains the PAN-2006 reconciliation: planning proceeds EVEN while draining the cohort, fills awaiting-release, never auto-starts, and never extends the running cohort
The rule contains the author/assignee gate and parked/objection exclusion as preconditions for planning each item, plus a capacity check against maxAgents
The rule contains the SLA: a ready, vetted, capacity-available issue is planned within ~1-2 ticks
